tiles.name: Montmartre
tiles.funFact: This hilltop village is crowned by the stunning white Sacré-Cœur Basilica!

tiles.name: Pigalle
tiles.funFact: Famous for the Moulin Rouge cabaret, founded in 1889!

tiles.name: Ligne 1
tiles.funFact: The first metro line in Paris, opened in 1900 for the World's Fair!

tiles.name: Bastille
tiles.funFact: Site of the famous fortress prison stormed during the French Revolution in 1789!

tiles.name: Marais
tiles.funFact: The historic Jewish quarter, now one of Paris's trendiest neighborhoods!

tiles.name: Luxembourg
tiles.funFact: The Luxembourg Gardens feature 106 statues and the beautiful Medici Fountain!

tiles.name: Saint-Germain
tiles.funFact: Historic literary café district where Hemingway and Sartre debated philosophy!

tiles.name: EDF
tiles.funFact: Électricité de France is the world's largest producer of electricity!

tiles.name: Latin Quarter
tiles.funFact: Home to the Sorbonne University, founded in 1257!

tiles.name: Panthéon
tiles.funFact: Final resting place of France's greatest minds including Voltaire and Rousseau!

tiles.name: Ligne 4
tiles.funFact: This line crosses the Seine twice and passes under Île de la Cité!

tiles.name: Versailles
tiles.funFact: The Palace of Versailles has 2,300 rooms and 67,000 square meters of floor space!

tiles.name: Tuileries
tiles.funFact: These royal gardens connect the Louvre to the Place de la Concorde!

tiles.name: Louvre
tiles.funFact: The world's largest art museum, home to the Mona Lisa and 38,000 artworks!

tiles.name: Opéra
tiles.funFact: The Palais Garnier opera house inspired "The Phantom of the Opera"!

tiles.name: Champs-Élysées
tiles.funFact: The most expensive retail street in Europe at €12,000 per square meter!

tiles.name: Arc de Triomphe
tiles.funFact: Built by Napoleon, it honors those who fought for France!

tiles.name: RER A
tiles.funFact: The busiest train line in Europe, carrying 1.2 million passengers daily!

tiles.name: La Défense
tiles.funFact: Europe's largest business district with the iconic Grande Arche!

tiles.name: Concorde
tiles.funFact: Where Louis XVI and Marie Antoinette were executed during the Revolution!

tiles.name: Veolia Water
tiles.funFact: Paris water comes from sources over 150km away, ensuring the finest quality!

tiles.name: Invalides
tiles.funFact: Napoleon's tomb lies under the golden dome of Les Invalides!

tiles.name: Île de la Cité
tiles.funFact: The historic heart of Paris, where the city was founded in 250 BC!

tiles.name: Notre-Dame
tiles.funFact: This Gothic masterpiece took 182 years to complete (1163-1345)!

tiles.name: Musée d'Orsay
tiles.funFact: Built in a former railway station, it houses the world's finest Impressionist collection!

tiles.name: Ligne 14
tiles.funFact: Paris's newest fully automated metro line, opened in 1998!

tiles.name: Montparnasse
tiles.funFact: Historic artist quarter where Picasso, Hemingway, and Fitzgerald gathered!

tiles.name: Eiffel Tower
tiles.funFact: The Iron Lady weighs 10,100 tons and was the world's tallest structure until 1930!
